items.title: Free dental clinic changes lives in cost-of-living crisis

children.content: Demand is high for an Adelaide dental program that offers free services to patients who have experienced homelessness or who have trouble accessing conventional care.

gallery.altText: A woman smiles as she holds a guitar across her lap, sitting in a park

thumbnail.altText: A woman smiles as she holds a guitar across her lap, sitting in a park

items.title: Woman who appeared 'belligerent' after fatal crash sentenced

children.content: A woman who was found to be mentally incompetent when she caused a fatal multi-car crash has been released into a transitional mental health facility.

image.altText: A woman with long dark hair wearing a black tshirt sits in a room at a desk. A green chair is behind her

items.title: Shooters deployed to curb Kangaroo Island's big little corella problem

children.content: Thermal imaging, infrared technology and crack marksmanship are deployed to battle the island's growing little corella population.

image.altText: A little corella in a tree with its wings slightly open

items.title: MP's extra voting paper for SA upper house among electoral problems under scrutiny

children.content: As authorities commit to a review of problems faced by South Australian voters during this month's election, a former Liberal MP says he was concerned to received an extra ballot paper in the post.

articles.title: One Nation claims seat of MacKillop, its third in South Australia's lower house

children.content: A former immigration detention system worker on Christmas Island has won the seat of MacKillop.

image.altText: A headshot of a man smiling at the camera.

gallery.altText: A collection of SA ballot papers including two for the Legislative Council.

thumbnail.altText: A collection of SA ballot papers including two for the Legislative Council.

items.title: Concerns about light and noise from $1b outback energy project resolved

children.content: The battery project, which will provide back-up power for up to 80,000 homes in Broken Hill, will go ahead with new conditions after a dispute was resolved between the company and an outback stargazing business.

image.altText: Aerial view of highway with a few residences pictured on the ground.

items.title: Businesses report increase in abuse from customers as fuel crisis worsens

children.content: From pile-on comments on social media to roadside slurs, local businesses are reminding their communities to "be kind to the little guy" amid the fuel crisis.

image.altText: a woman stares at the camera with a houseboat in the background

items.title: Coroner finds paramedic who made 'man flu' comments failed to meet standards

children.content: A coroner has recommended the South Australian Ambulance Service consider implementing an inclusive language program after an inquest heard an Indian national was told he was overreacting with the "man flu" in the days before he died of chronic pneumonia.

image.altText: Paramedic in green work clothes with grey hair stands next to ambulance

items.title: South Australia wins back-to-back Shield titles with stunning win over Victoria

children.content: South Australia has won back-to-back Sheffield Shield titles with a brilliant comeback victory over Victoria in the final at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

image.altText: South Australia bowler Henry Thornton jumps, shouts and punches the air to celebrate a Sheffield Shield wicket.

items.title: Artists and scientists unite to throw spotlight on seaweed's 'beauty'

children.content: South Australia's rugged south-east coast is known for its cold water smashing into limestone cliffs. But beneath the surface is a global hotspot of seaweed biodiversity.

image.altText: An underwater shot of colourful seaweed.

items.title: SA rules out free public transport, for now, as dozens of servos are out of some fuels

children.content: The South Australian government has no plans to follow other states in offering free public transport despite more than 70 service stations running out of at least one fuel type.

image.altText: A tram at a level crossing with a bus waiting behind boomgates

items.title: 36ers lodge complaint to NBL over Bogut's 'personal attacks' on owner

children.content: Drama from the spiteful championship series between the Adelaide 36ers and Sydney Kings goes off the court as the 36ers complain to the NBL about the conduct of Kings co-owner and assistant Andrew Bogut.

image.altText: Andrew Bogut points with his left hand

items.title: Childhood vaccine coverage declines for fifth year in a row

children.content: The latest immunisation data shows more than 80,000 Australian children aged five and under are not fully up to date with their vaccinations.

image.altText: A nurse holds a needle up to a young boy's arm

items.title: Dezi Freeman died isolated from the modern world, but not far from it

children.content: The fugitive resurfaced more than 150 kilometres away from the town where he shot and killed two police officers seven months ago. Mystified locals are questioning how.

articles.title: 'Evil inside him': Dezi Freeman's nephew speaks out on cop killer uncle's death

children.content: When Dezi Freeman was shot dead, it left his family with mixed emotions. Some are shattered, others know an "evil man" is dead. His nephew speaks out.

image.altText: Two men smiling for a photo in a backyard.

articles.title: Freeman joins a long line of fugitives captured or killed in Australia

children.content: Australia has a long history of violent outlaws heading bush to avoid capture. But how is it still possible to remain on the run for so long?

image.altText: A photo of Dezi and an image of Ned Kelly

gallery.altText: A road leading through Thologolong, with a blue and white sign beside the tree-lined road.
A police car is parked in front of the large Thologolong property where Dezi Freeman was found.
A mountain slope can be seen behind burnt-out trees.

thumbnail.altText: A road leading through Thologolong, with a blue and white sign beside the tree-lined road.
A police car is parked in front of the large Thologolong property where Dezi Freeman was found.
A mountain slope can be seen behind burnt-out trees.

items.title: Mine may be shut down amid money laundering allegations, bikie claims

children.content: The Tasmanian government has taken steps to revoke the operating licence of one of the state's biggest mining operations, after being warned it was allegedly used as a "vehicle for money laundering" and former owners were associated with underworld figures.

image.altText: An aerial of mining buildings in a forest and huge mountains in the background.

items.title: Less than half of states signed up as national gun reform deadline passes

children.content: The federal government's March deadline for national gun reform has come and gone, with more than half the states and territories still opposed to parts of the plan. Only four have committed to a gun buyback scheme.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese wearing glasses and looking stern.

items.title: 'Nothing's out here' Vodafone ad angers rural Australians

children.content: A new Vodafone campaign has regional Australians seething after the telecommunications giant claimed to focus its network infrastructure on where "98 whatever per cent of you actually live".

gallery.altText: A screengrab of an ad campaign featuring an emu standing in the outback next to a woman in a red dress

thumbnail.altText: A screengrab of an ad campaign featuring an emu standing in the outback next to a woman in a red dress

items.title: ASX jumps on back of massive Wall St rally amid war end hopes

children.content: Wall Street ends a weak month with a massive gain, as investors eye a possible end to US military action in Iran, and Australian shares follow. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A close up of Donald Trump.

items.title: Chalmers demands states resolve GST fuel spat 'ASAP'

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers wants the states to follow through on the agreement to find a way to return windfall GST revenue raised from higher fuel prices. Follow live.

image.altText: chalmers

items.title: Developer fined for chopping trees plans to clear more for $10m mansion

children.content: A property developer who illegally cut down hundreds of native trees has lodged a new plan to build a lavish $10 million, six-bedroom mansion in Sydney's south.

gallery.altText: An alleged tree cleared area on a hill on a sunny day.

thumbnail.altText: An alleged tree cleared area on a hill on a sunny day.

avatar.alt: A man in black shirt against a dark background

items.title: Woods to seek treatment after pleading not guilty to DUI

children.content: Tiger Woods says he will step away and seek treatment, days after he was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence.

image.altText: Golfer Tiger Woods stands by his overturned vehicle

items.title: Socceroos headliners shine, but will the experimentation pay off at World Cup?

children.content: The Socceroos are done with experimenting, but now need to find the winning formula with the World Cup quickly approaching.

image.altText: Socceroos player Nestory Irankunda holds his crotch with one hand and points other hand with silver glove

items.title: Travel quiz: Get out your passport and see if you can beat our quiz

children.content: Which is Australia’s oldest Luna Park? And where would you find Elvis Presley’s mansion?

items.title: Could I be using E10 in my car?

children.content: Australia's leading motoring organisation is urging drivers to fill up with E10 fuel in an effort to conserve national fuel supplies. But what exactly is E10? And could it help ease the current fuel crisis?

image.altText: A man holds a petrol bowser while filling up his car.

items.title: This controversial rom-com starring Zendaya is one of the best movies of the year

children.content: On the surface, A-listers Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new film seems like a regular rom-com. That's exactly what director Kristoffer Borgli wants you to think.

image.altText: A man dips a woman while they smile at each other

items.title: House prices rise everywhere besides Sydney and Melbourne

children.content: There is a significant divide between property price growth in major cities as buyers weigh up interest rate hikes and geopolitical tensions.

items.title: One of Australia's biggest 'giveaway' clubs has its licence cancelled

children.content: RS Rewards claims to have given away more than $10 million worth of cars, houses and cash. But its licence has just been cancelled.

image.altText: A luxurious house on the gold coast with a large pool, surrounded by palm trees.

items.title: Cocoa prices crash but Easter chocolate stays expensive

children.content: Consumers cannot expect to find cheaper chocolate on supermarket shelves this Easter despite a major drop in the world cocoa price, industry experts say.

image.altText: Close up of small easter eggs in a bowl.

items.title: Calls for tourism lease of world heritage area island to be cancelled

children.content: A private developer who wants to build a standing camp on an island in the Tasmanian Wilderness World Heritage Area tells the ABC he has requested another lease extension, amid an outcry from the Tasmanian Greens.

image.altText: A picturesque lake on a cloudy day. The far horizon is slightly obscured by fog, but trees are visible closer to the forefront

items.title: Aussie teen star gets Billie Jean King Cup call-up

children.content: At just 17, Emerson Jones is a chance to make her Billie Jean King Cup debut after being named in Australia's squad to face Great Britain.

image.altText: Emerson Jones plays a shot

items.title: Shein and Kmart accused of copying Queensland retailer's designs

children.content: Queensland business Sabo Skirt has claimed Shein, Kmart and numerous other retailers and wholesalers have copied its clothing patterns, prints and trademarks on "lower quality" garments in a federal court claim.

gallery.altText: A graphic with a dress with an aquatic print and a Sabo logo next to Shein and Kmart logos over aquatic print garments.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic with a dress with an aquatic print and a Sabo logo next to Shein and Kmart logos over aquatic print garments.

items.title: Jewish people scared to join Bondi inquiry, former royal commissioner says

children.content: Ronald Sackville who led the disability royal commission says it is unlikely the the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion can properly examine the experiences of Jewish Australians before its December 2026 deadline.

image.altText: Floral tributes on promenade at Bondi Beach

items.title: Three men charged over daylight shooting outside Sydney gym

children.content: The alleged target, a 48-year-old man known to police, had twelve rounds fired at him outside a gym in December last year.

image.altText: Police tape across a car park outside a world gym.

items.title: Japan deploys first long-range missiles capable of reaching mainland China

children.content: The upgraded Type-12 missile has a range of about 1000 km, allowing it to now reach mainland China.

image.altText: The Type-12 land-to-ship missile launcher is shown at the Camp Kengun in the Kumamoto prefecture

items.title: Israeli death penalty discriminatory and potentially a 'war crime', says UN

children.content: UN rights chief Volker Turk calls for the bill to be "promptly repealed", warning it is "patently inconsistent with Israel's international law obligations".

image.altText: Crowds of people clamouring around a bus waving.

items.title: New shark species discovered in 'unexplored' deep-sea waters off Qld

children.content: More than 110 new fish and invertebrate species are discovered during a CSIRO voyage in the deep Coral Sea.
